general_info.definition: Polyethyleneimine (PEI)-functionalized carbon dots (p-CDs) synthesized via hydrothermal method, exhibiting strong blue fluorescence. Used as a fluorescent component in a nanoassembly with MnO₂ nanosheets for signal amplification in fluorescence immunoassays.

method.conditions: Citric acid (0.30 g), branched PEI (0.80 g) and 20mL water were mixed then heated at 180°C for 6 hours in a 50mL Teflon-lined autoclave

post_treatment: Dialysis against distilled water using 8000 Da membrane for 48 h

quantum_yield.reference_material: integrating sphere attached to an FS-5 spectrofluorometer

structural.crystallinity: Graphitic structure with a lattice spacing of 0.30 nm which matches with the (002) lattice spacing of graphite.

applications.performance: Linear detection for AMD in the range of 0.048-1.1 ng mL-1; Limit of Detection (LOD): 0.035 ng mL-1. Recovery in spiked chicken samples: 72.6-90.4% with coefficient of variation <13.2%.
applications.comparative_advantage: Good selectivity and specificity. Great potential to be applied in detecting trace chemical contaminants in food.

feedback.label: Regular
feedback.expert_comment: Omission Error: The field 'carbon_dots -> properties -> optical -> excitation_max' contained '360nm' in the source text, but it was extracted as null/missing.
